name: Friesland (Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: the colour of the four wider diagonal bands
the colour of the three narrower diagonal bands
the colour of the seven pompeblêden

description: Four blue and three white diagonal bands running from the upper hoist to the lower fly, charged with seven red pompeblêden (heart-shaped water-lily leaves), one centred on each white band.
history: Adopted by the provincial states on 9 July 1957, based on traditional Frisian heraldic banners going back to the medieval period; the seven leaves represent the seven historic Frisian Sea Lands.

features.symbolism: the seven blue-and-white diagonal bands evoke the historic banners of the medieval seven Frisian Sea Lands (Sjelannen)
seven red pompeblêden, each representing one of the historic Frisian Sea Lands; each leaf is placed centred on one of the white diagonal bands
